mentions.note: Managed care play up on same Medicare Advantage tailwinds as Humana; legendary CEO Steve Hemsley back turning around brutal prior management. Own it.

Managed care play up for same reason as Humana (Medicare Advantage reimbursements). Legendary CEO Steve Hemsley back and turning around brutal prior management. Buy it; Cramer crushed it buying UNH in 1988.
Managed care beneficiary of Trump Medicare Advantage breaks; CEO Steve Hemsley turning around brutal legacy mismanagement. Textbook safety stock.
Managed care leader. Beneficiary of Medicare Advantage reimbursement boost. CEO Steve Hemsley back and turning around prior bad management. Cramer bought it in 1988 and crushed it. Buy now.
Legendary CEO Steve Hemsley back after brutal mismanagement. Managed care play benefiting from Medicare Advantage. Cramer owned it in 1988 and crushed it; buy now.
Healthcare insurance leader; part of emerging healthcare leadership group offering better growth and valuation than tech.

Returning CEO Steve Hemsley produced best managed care earnings in years; first solid beat-and-raise in long time. Stock fell from $600 (April 2025) to $354; trades at 19x despite strong execution. Major upside ahead.
Steve Hemsley return delivering upside surprises as expected. Balance sheet solid, management restored. Expect many quarters of outperformance. Stock up 7%.
Once pristine insurer fighting back to legitimacy; reports Tuesday; series of big earnings to follow.
Medicare Advantage rates renegotiation imminent; volatility risk paralleled Centene's $7 drop; too topsy-turvy despite strong Optum division; avoid despite fundamentals.
Medicare Advantage rate increase of only 0.09% vs. 4-5% expected crushes margins; stock fell 19% on regulatory disappointment.
Dividend growth stock hammered; low enough to buy but CVS better entry with higher yield and Aetna asset.
